name: Warm 'n Spicy Apple Roly-Poly
recipeIngredient: 1 large tart cooking apple (such as Granny Smith), peeled, cored, and chopped into 1-1/2 cups
2 3/4 cups variety baking and pancake mix (such as Bisquick)
1/2 tsp pumpkin pie spice or ground cinnamon
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 400 degrees F (200 degrees C). In a 13 x 9 x 2-inch baking pan, stir together the reserved liquid from the apple pie filling, apple juice, and grated lemon peel.
2. In a medium bowl, combine the apple pie filling apples, tart cooking apple, pecans, and raisins; mix well.
3. In a large bowl, combine the baking mix, granulated sugar, and spice with milk to form a stiff, firm dough. Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface and knead two or three times until smooth.
4. On a clean, floured surface, roll the dough out to a 12-inch square. Spoon the apple mixture over the dough, leaving a 1/2-inch border on all sides. Roll up the dough jelly-roll style. Using a serrated knife, cut the roll into twelve 1-inch crosswise slices.
5. Place the slices in the baking pan with their cut side down, spacing them evenly apart. Pour liquid around the rolls. Bake for 40 minutes until the rolls are puffed and browned and the syrup is bubbling. Remove from the wire rack to cool about 20 minutes.
6. Sprinkle the rolls with confectioners' sugar while still warm. Drizzle with fudge sauce from the tip of a spoon. Serve warm.
